steps.title: Identify the scope difference
steps.content: Fodor's theory limits modularity to certain cognitive systems (specifically low-level, excluding reasoning), while Carruthers's MMH extends it to all cognitive systems — the core signal is the difference in scope.

steps.title: Match the scope contrast
steps.content: A directly uses “some but not all” to describe Fodor's theory and emphasizes MMH's greater “expansiveness” — capturing the scope difference from the notes. The other three choices either wrongly suggest sequential causation, falsely claim the theories differ where they agree, or misidentify the basis of disagreement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
